channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLbusinessAndMarkets, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: ‘I have a chip on my shoulder.’ Phoebe Gates wants her $185 million AI startup Phia to succeed with ‘no ties to my privilege or my last name’
item.description: The daughter of Bill Gates and Melinda French Gates acknowledges her privilege, but says she’s building her own thing without their help.

item.title: Burger King wants you to call its president to complain. No, really
item.description: I called Burger King president Tom Curtis a few times this week, but it went straight to voicemail. You can try too, his number is (305) 874-0520. Oka...

item.title: Why Clarity Might Be the Most Underrated Leadership Advantage
item.description: Clarity isn’t just a communication skill. It’s a competitive edge in leadership.

item.title: The CEO of a $1 billion AI unicorn says his peers in Silicon Valley want you to fear for your job, but they’re actually first on the chopping block
item.description: Tanmai Gopal of PromptQL said tech people have a bad habit of thinking "this affects me. So it's going to affect everyone like that."

item.title: The secret to all those death-defying Olympic jumps is a giant plastic airbag
item.description: The highlight reel of the Milan Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics was defined by extreme tricks—corkscrews, twists, and flips performed by snowboarders and...

item.title: This Olympic skill can boost your job performance
item.description: Olympians aren’t just physically exceptional—they’re masters at managing where their attention and energy go. Cognitive research finds a key link betw...

item.title: Employees Are Walking Away From Higher Pay. Here’s Why HR Didn’t See it Coming
item.description: Compensation still matters, but for many employees, benefits are doing the real work of retention.

item.title: How fandom became culture’s power center — and a blueprint for Gen Z’s economic influence
item.description: Fandoms sit at the intersection of identity, influence, and spending. Three dynamics explain their accelerating economic power.

item.title: How to prepare for a market crash
item.description: How are you feeling about the stock market these days? Depending on who you talk to, the American economy is either poised to soar into the stratosphe...

item.title: Bad Bunny Highlighted Puerto Rico’s Energy Crisis. This Stanford Duo is Doing Something About it
item.description: Raya Power co-founders Meghan Wood and Nicole Gonzalez want to make solar power as accessible as buying and installing a fridge.

item.title: 3 Tips for Customer Service Recovery
item.description: Your worst moments will become your greatest opportunities for building true customer loyalty.

item.title: Peter Thiel and other tech billionaires are publicly shielding their children from the products that made them rich
item.description: As governments race to restrict minors’ social media use, the tech billionaires who built the platforms are imposing strict screen limits at home.

item.title: If the recent AI and crypto shocks upset you, you’re tracking the wrong cycle
item.description: Claude and DeepSeek, separated at birth?

item.title: Why using facial recognition on your phone could leave you vulnerable
item.description: Biometric authentication—the ability to unlock your devices by using just your face or fingerprint—is one of the few smartphone features that, even to...

item.title: These Are the 7 Highest Paying Side Hustles for 2026, According to a New Report
item.description: A new report ranked side hustles by potential income. Here are the ones that topped the list for 2026.

item.title: Why Nvidia is ‘highly motivated’ to keep investing in OpenAI
This once-hot cancer-detection company’s stock fell 50% after a failed trial
Prediction-market ETFs may open the door to all sorts of Wall Street tomfoolery
Dow gains 230 points, stocks end higher after SCOTUS rules against Trump tariffs
Don’t expect lower prices now that the Supreme Court struck down Trump’s tariffs
